title: rubus aculeolatus var. iuvenis
intro: Rubus aculeolatus var. iuvenis, also known as the Thimble Bramble, is a variant of the Blackberry plant, characterized by its edible berries and thorny stems. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Rubus aculeolatus var. iuvenis include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection against cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often made by infusing the leaves, stems, and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
